meta.release_notes:
# Changelog

All notable changes to this model will be documented in this file.

## [2.0.0] 26.03

### Changed

- introduced `io.catenax.shared.industry_core.common:1.0.0` and `io.catenax.shared.industry_core.part_classification:1.0.0` as shared models
- renamed `catenaXId` to `globalAssetId`
- renamed `catenaXsiteId` to `siteId`
- renamed `classificationID` to `classificationId`

## [1.0.0] 2024-02-12

- Initial version of the aspect model PartTypeInformation (formerly PartAsPlanned)

nodes.label: PartTypeInformation
nodes.description: A Part Type Information represents an item in the Catena-X Bill of Material (BOM) on a type level in a specific version.

nodes.label: catenaXId
nodes.description: The fully anonymous Catena-X ID of the serialized part, valid for the Catena-X dataspace.

nodes.label: partTypeInformation
nodes.description: The part type from which the serialized part has been instantiated.

nodes.label: partSitesInformationAsPlanned
nodes.description: A site is a delimited geographical area where a legal entity does business (geographical address with geo coordinates). A site always has a primary physical address. It is possible that further physical addresses are specified for the site. P.O. box details are only possible in addition to the physical address. A site has a 1:n relation to addresses, means at least one address is necessary and multiple addresses are possible.

nodes.label: manufacturerPartId
nodes.description: Part ID as assigned by the manufacturer of the part. The part ID identifies the part in the manufacturer`s dataspace. The part ID references a specific version of a part. The version number must be included in the part ID if it is available. The part ID does not reference a specific instance of a part and must not be confused with the serial number.

nodes.label: nameAtManufacturer
nodes.description: Name of the part as assigned by the manufacturer.

nodes.label: catenaXsiteId
nodes.description: The identifier of the site according to Catena-X BPDM. The catenaXsiteId must be a valid Catena-X BPN. The BPN is a unique, unchangeable identifier for business partners / company locations from foundation to closure, regardless of the different business relationships / structures between or within the business partners or company locations.

nodes.label: function
nodes.description: The function of the site in relation to the part (i.e. the activity within the value chain of the part that is performed at the site).

nodes.label: functionValidFrom
nodes.description: Timestamp, from when the site has the specified function for the given part.

nodes.label: functionValidUntil
nodes.description: Timestamp, until when the site has the specified function for the given part.

nodes.label: PartTypeInformationCharacteristic
nodes.description: The characteristics of the part type.

nodes.label: PartIdCharacteristic
nodes.description: The part ID is a multi-character string, usually assigned by an ERP system.

nodes.label: PartNameCharacteristic
nodes.description: Part name in string format from the respective system in the value chain.

nodes.label: DateTimeCharacteristic
nodes.description: Describes a property which contains the date and time with an optional timezone.

nodes.label: partSitesInformationAsPlannedCharacteristic
nodes.preferredName: Part Sites Information as Planned Characteristic
nodes.description: The characteristic of the part site information that represents a set of possible site to a respective part.

nodes.label: PartTypeInformationEntity
nodes.description: Encapsulation for data related to the part type.

nodes.label: partSitesInformationAsPlannedEntity
nodes.preferredName: Part Sites Information as Planned Entity
nodes.description: Describes the ID, function and validity date of a site for the associated part in the AsPlanned context.

nodes.label: FunctionCharacteristic
nodes.description: Describes the characteristics of the function for a site related to the respective part.

nodes.label: DateTrait
nodes.description: Trait to ensure regular expressions with different date formats.

nodes.label: DateTimeRegularExpression
nodes.description: Regular Expression to enable UTC and Timezone formats and the possibility to exclude time information.
